title: The Judge Returns
subtitle: The Final Judgement in the Valley of Josaphat — when Christ appears, gentle to the just and terrible to the unjust

stillness: Be still, and think often upon this judgement.
Let the thought of so great a dread strike fear into you,
and let that fear drive out the sin you have done,
and hold you back from sinning again.
As Jerome said: whether I eat, or drink, or do anything else,
always that terrible voice seems to sound in my ears:
"Rise, you dead, and come to judgement."
gospel: Behold, the Bridegroom comes; go out to meet him.
He will not come when he is looked for,
but at midnight — secretly and unawares.
And the King will say to those on his right:
I was hungry, and you gave me to eat;
I was thirsty, and you gave me to drink;
I was a stranger, and you took me in;
I was in prison, and you came to me.
But to those on his left he will say:
I was hungry, and you gave me nothing to eat.
And they will begin to say to the mountains and the rocks:
Fall upon us, and hide us from the face of him who sits upon the throne,
and from the wrath of the Lamb.
behold: After the resurrection, at once will come the judgement.
It will be in the valley of Josaphat,
which lies to the east, between Jerusalem and the Mount of Olives.
For Josaphat means "the judgement of the Lord."
Christ will not come down to the earth to judge,
but will be seated in the air, facing the Mount of Olives, from which he ascended,
thronged about by the hosts and the Virtues of the heavens.
And that judgement will be horrible on every side:
on the left, countless demons terrifying;
all around, the good angels driving into hell, and the evil ones dragging there.
He will show the marks too, and the signs of the Passion:
the cross, the nails, the lance, the crown, and the scourges.
And then Christ the Lord will appear gentle to the just,
consider: Consider that you, O man, sinned in soul and in body together;
and so in both together you shall be punished, or in both rewarded.
The wicked shall rise deformed and able to suffer.
So shameful will the body of the sinner be,
that the soul taking it up again will be aghast when it sees it,
and will shudder to look upon it,
and would rather have such a body as it was
when it was half-eaten by the worms.
But in the good, nature will be kept and the vices stripped away,
and the body will be seven times brighter than the sun is now.
Then the books of consciences will be laid open,
and that book which is called the Book of Life.
All the secrets of all will lie bare to all,
with so great a clearness of certainty,
that no path of concealing, defending, excusing, or escaping will lie open,
but each will receive according to his deserts.
Then hear the Judge, now more just than merciful, accuse the guilty, and say:
I made you, O man, from the mud, with my own hands;
I poured a spirit into limbs of earth;
I set you among the delights of paradise.
But you chose to follow the deceiver rather than the Lord.
Behold the marks of the nails, by which I hung fixed;
behold the side pierced with wounds.
I took up your sorrows, to give you my glory;
I took up your death, that you might live forever.
Why have you lost what I bore for you?
Why, ungrateful one, have you refused the gifts of your redemption?
Heavier with me is the cross of your sins, on which I hang bound,
than the one on which, in pity for you, I climbed to die your death.
Give me back your life, for which I gave my own.
It is better here to bear a little bitterness,
and afterward to come to eternal sweetness,
than here to have a false joy,
and there to receive torment without end.
prayer: who through the boundless patience of your goodness
grant to me, a wretched man,
that I may judge myself now, while I examine myself,
and may have strength to shun all that displeases you,
and to fulfill all that pleases you;
and so may I become pleasing and acceptable to you,
that in the judgement to come,
set with your elect on the right hand,
and finding you favorable and appeased,
I may receive the kingdom prepared for them,
to enjoy forever your longed-for presence,
my desire, my reward, and my life. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Et erit judicium illud horribile ex omni parte: supra, erit Judex iratus; subtus, patens infernus; intus conscientia remordens; extra, mundus ardens.
latinJewel.english: And that judgement will be horrible on every side: above, the angry Judge; below, hell lying open; within, conscience gnawing; without, the world burning.
